audits.evidence: test_01 tx_single_byte passes. TX FSM emits start+data+stop.
test_03 rx_single_byte passes. RX oversample mid-bit sampling works.
test_04 tx_parity passes. TX computes and sends parity bit.
test_05 verifies parity check logic; even parity data path verified in test_03.
test_05 parity error detected correctly with wrong parity bit.
test_06 frame error detected with bad stop bit.
test_07 overrun error when FIFO full and another byte received.
TX FSM waits for fifo_empty=0 before starting; no underrun possible.
test_02 and test_13 loopback passes with multiple patterns.
test_14 break send verified: TX line held low when break asserted in IDLE.
RTL has stop_bits_i support but no dedicated testbench test. Code path verified by inspection.
test_09 verifies tx_empty interrupt fires and W1C clears.
RTL has rx_not_empty_en and edge-triggered pending. No dedicated test.
DATA_WIDTH is parameterized but always 8 in simulation.
test_08 verifies tx_empty, tx_full flags via STAT register.
RX START_DETECT state confirms start bit at mid-sample before advancing.
BAUD register is RW (test_12). Dynamic change not tested mid-stream.
test_12 register RW: CTRL write/read verified.
test_01, test_08 read STAT successfully.
test_12 BAUD write/read verified.
tx_push in test_01 writes TXDATA; data appears on TX output.
rx_pop in test_03 reads RXDATA; correct data returned.
test_12 INTEN write/read verified.
test_09 verifies INTPEND W1C clear.
test_10 verifies CLR_STAT clears frame_err.
test_01 reads DBG_BYTES_TX, test_02 reads DBG_BYTES_RX.
test_11 verifies PSLVERR for address >= 0x30.
All APB transactions complete with PREADY=1 in access phase.
No explicit reserved field test. Register map has no gaps.
tx_o default is 1'b1 in IDLE. test_01 verifies TX completes to idle.
TX shifts out tx_shift_reg[0] (LSB first). test_01 verifies 0xA5 output.
RTL has rx_sync_1/rx_sync_2 2-FF synchronizer for external path.
mid_sample fires at (baud_div+1)*(OVERSAMPLE/2) = half-bit. test_03 confirms correct sampling.
TX baud counter wraps at (baud_div+1)*OVERSAMPLE-1. Period verified by test_01.
No dedicated test for mid-stream baud change.
test_14 verifies break holds TX low for multiple bit periods.
Loopback uses direct tx path (same clock domain, no sync delay). test_02 passes.
uart_irq_o = OR of (pend & en) for all sources. test_09 verifies irq assertion.
irq is active-high. test_09 checks dut.uart_irq == 1.
RX pushes byte even on frame error (STOP1 state pushes regardless of frame_err).
RX pushes byte even on parity error. test_05 verifies rx_pop returns data.
When FIFO full, overrun_err set and byte NOT pushed. test_07 verifies.
underrun tied to 0 in core. No underrun possible in this design.
Sticky error flags latched until CLR_STAT write. test_10 verifies.
32-bit counters wrap naturally. No explicit wrap test.
test reset() deasserts PRESETn; all registers/FIFOs/state cleared.
